commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Telegram active-run ingress sequential lane for plain-text follow-ups in direct and isolated polling paths, documents it, and adds a runtime file SecretRef fallback for bare root keys.

PR surface: Source +159, Tests +176, Docs 0. Total +335 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: no. live current-main reproduction was run, but source inspection is high-confidence: current main serializes Telegram updates by getTelegramSequentialKey in both direct and spool paths, and file JSON SecretRefs require absolute JSON pointers.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the contributor must supply PR-branch Telegram proof and maintainers/secops must decide the SecretRef runtime compatibility exception.

target.title: [codex] Fix Telegram active-run ingress and legacy file SecretRefs
target.body:
## Summary
- route plain-text Telegram follow-ups in an active reply fence through a narrow ingress lane so they can reach queue/steer handling without waiting behind the running handler
- share that active-run lane selection between the direct grammY runner and isolated-spool polling drain path
- keep commands, media, and media-group Telegram updates on the normal serialized lane for compatibility
- keep Telegram sequential-key typing structural around the fields it actually reads, so real bot contexts and isolated-spool/test contexts stay type-compatible
- resolve legacy bare root-key file SecretRefs when the JSON payload has that exact root property, while preserving JSON-pointer schema/parity checks

## Root Cause
Telegram used one per-chat/per-thread sequential lane for all inbound updates, so follow-up text sent during an active run waited for the current handler to finish before it could affect the run. The first patch fixed the direct grammY sequentializer, but isolated polling uses its own durable spool drain and was still deriving lanes with the old serialized key. Separately, older managed secrets configs can contain file SecretRef ids that name root JSON keys directly; newer runtime resolution required JSON pointers and could fail gateway startup before users had a chance to migrate.

## Validation
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.test.ts src/secrets/resolve.test.ts src/secrets/exec-secret-ref-id-parity.test.ts src/config/config.secrets-schema.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -b tsconfig.projects.json`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/telegram/src/active-run-ingress.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-core.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.ts extensions/telegram/src/sequential-key.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/telegram-reply-fence.ts src/secrets/resolve.ts src/secrets/resolve.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

## Notes
- Latest upstream already treats ChatGPT/Codex auth as the `openai` provider with `CODEX_API_KEY`; I did not carry the older local `openai-codex` env-var hunk into this PR.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** OpenClaw gateway startup recovered from the stale file-backed SecretRef compatibility failure, and Telegram/Slack channel health remained live after the compatibility repair. The Telegram active-run ingress behavior is covered by focused tests for both the direct Telegram middleware path and the isolated-spool polling path; commands/media remain on the normal serialized lane.
- **Real environment tested:** Local macOS OpenClaw setup on Hanson's Mini, OpenClaw runtime `2026.5.28`, gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789`, Telegram polling accounts `clawd2` through `clawd6` plus `default`, Slack `compbioatberkeley`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran live `openclaw gateway status --json`, `openclaw status --json`, and `openclaw gateway call channels.status --json --timeout 30000 --params '{}'` after repairing the stale SecretRefs and restarting the gateway.
- **Evidence after fix:** Live terminal output, redacted to omit secret values:
```json
{
  "gatewayStatus": {
    "runtime": { "status": "running", "state": "active", "pid": 60970 },
    "rpc": { "ok": true, "kind": "connect", "version": "2026.5.28" },
    "secretDiagnostics": null
  },
  "openclawStatus": {
    "runtimeVersion": "2026.5.28",
    "gatewayReachable": true,
    "gatewayError": null,
    "gatewayService": { "status": "running", "state": "active", "pid": 60970 },
    "nodeService": { "status": "running", "state": "active", "pid": 3652 },
    "taskAudit": { "total": 0, "warnings": 0, "errors": 0 },
    "secretDiagnostics": []
  },
  "channelsStatus": {
    "eventLoopDegraded": false,
    "telegram": [
      { "accountId": "clawd2", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "tokenStatus": "available", "mode": "polling" },
      { "accountId": "clawd3", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "tokenStatus": "available", "mode": "polling" },
      { "accountId": "clawd4", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "tokenStatus": "available", "mode": "polling" },
      { "accountId": "clawd5", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "tokenStatus": "available", "mode": "polling" },
      { "accountId": "clawd6", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "tokenStatus": "available", "mode": "polling" },
      { "accountId": "default", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "tokenStatus": "available", "mode": "polling" }
    ],
    "slack": [
      { "accountId": "compbioatberkeley", "running": true, "connected": true, "lastError": null, "botTokenStatus": "available", "appTokenStatus": "available" }
    ]
  }
}
```
- **Observed result after fix:** Gateway RPC returned `ok: true`, `secretDiagnostics` was empty/null, task audit had `0` warnings and `0` errors, all six Telegram accounts were `running: true` and `connected: true` with no last error, and Slack `compbioatberkeley` was connected with both token statuses available.
- **What was not tested:** I did not exercise the PR branch against a live Telegram active-run from this checkout because the installed local runtime is `2026.5.28`; the live incident showed the installed isolated-spool runtime can still backlog until this PR is merged or locally installed.
target.author: hilo-hilo
